title: RadioButton Properties
description:
RadioButton Properties

RadioButton Properties and Events

The following list is a summary of the RadioButton control properties with brief descriptions.  Click on the property names for more details.

Property	Description

BackColor	Selects the background color for the
		 object's label.

Default: Control

Checked	Determines if the RadioButton control
		 is checked or not.

Default: 0 -
		 False

Enabled	Determines if the control is enabled
		 or not.

Default: 1 -
		 True

EventTaskType	The type of task used for events.

Default: 0 -
		 Normal

Font	Selects the font for the text in the
		 control.

Default: Microsoft
		 Sans Serif, 8.25pt.

FontBold	The bold attribute for the current font
		 (Only available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: 0 - False

FontItalic	The italic attribute for the current
		 font (Only available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: 0 - False

FontName	The name for the current font (Only
		 available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: Microsoft Sans Serif

FontSize	The size for the current font in points
		 (Only available from SPEL+ programs).

Default: 8.25

ForeColor	Selects the forecolor for the control.

Default: ControlText

Height	Specifies the height of the control.

Image	The picture for the control.  To
		 delete, press the [Delete] key.

Default: Empty

ImageAlign	Gets or sets the alignment of an image
		 in the control.

Default: 5 -
		 MiddleCenter

Left	Specifies
		 the left location of the control.

Name	Used to assign a unique name to the
		 RadioButton control.

Default: RadioButtonXX

TabIndex	Sets the tab index for the control

Default: 0

Text	Sets the text for the RadioButton control.

Default: Name
		 of control

TextAlign	Determines alignment for the text.

Default: 1 -
		 Left

ToolTipText	Sets the text
		 for the tooltip.

Default: Empty

Top	Sets the location of the top of the
		 RadioButton control.

Visible	Sets if the
		 control is Visible or not.

Default: 1 -
		 True

Width	Sets the width
		 of the RadioButton control.

Event	Description

Click	Executes when the user clicks the control
		 with the mouse.
